creator: cschuber@uumail.gov.bc.ca
text:
The id string from bsd.port.mk is:

#       $Id: bsd.port.mk,v 1.227.2.60 1998/11/11 05:23:19 asami Exp $
#       $NetBSD: $

The id string from the krb5 port's makefile is:

# $Id: Makefile,v 1.7 1998/11/07 02:33:44 nectar Exp $

bsd.port.pre.mk and bsd.port.post.mk have also been installed.

I've tried this on two 2.2.7-RELEASE systems one with CAM patches the 
other without.  The results are the same.

The only other thing I can think of is that I already have krb5 
installed with Dima's patches and those shared libraries are in the 
standard ld.so search path.  I will reboot without the existing krb5 
installed (e.g. renamed and /usr/local/krb5/lib removed from the ld.so 
search path) and try it again this weekend.


Regards,                       Phone:  (250)387-8437
